hook.text: What if every patient could be their own control? Crossover designs make that happen.

buildup.text: In a crossover trial, each participant receives both treatments — one after the other, in random order.

discovery.text: Because you compare each person to themselves, individual variation is eliminated. Smaller samples suffice.

twist.text: The washout period between treatments must be long enough to clear the first treatment's effects completely.

climax.text: Carryover effects can ruin a crossover design. If treatment A affects how B works, results become unreliable.

punchline.text: Be your own control. But only if the first treatment fully washes out.

quiz.question: What is the main advantage of a crossover trial design?
options: Each participant serves as their own control, eliminating individual variation
